Value Score — how cheap the listing is relative to comparable properties in the same City + Type segment (100 = cheapest in segment).  Desirability — how in-demand the segment is, proxied by median Price/m² (100 = most premium).  Gem Score — combined; a high score means cheap AND in a sought-after market.

#TypeCityCompound BedsArea m²Price (EGP M) Price/m²Seg. Median/m² ValueDesirabilityGem Score
Methodology
Market Segmentation

Properties grouped by City + Property Type. Value assessed relative to direct peers — not the overall market. Segments with fewer than 5 listings are excluded.

Value Score

Computed as 1 − percentile rank by Price/m² within the segment. Only listings in the bottom 25th percentile qualify as hidden gems.

Desirability Score

Based on the segment median Price/m², normalised 0–100. Prevents flagging cheap properties in low-demand markets that are cheap for good reason.

Inca Tribe — Hidden Gem Detector  |  Trained on 26,089 Egyptian property listings  |  Methodology: bottom-25th-percentile Price/m² within City+Type segment, scored by value and market desirability
